108.status: Was removed from April 25 game after taking a foul tip off his left wrist. Testing revealed the fracture.
Threw on April 24 in Arizona and felt good. Next steps will be determined after evaluating how he recovers.
Slated to throw an inning on April 25 for Triple-A Salt Lake. Continuing to build back up and has had no complications during rehab assignment.
Progressing well in throwing program. Has faced hitters twice and the Angels are determining how many more times he'll need to throw in simulated games before starting a rehab assignment.

Made a rehab start in a Minor League game in Arizona but exited with hamstring discomfort after throwing two pitches on April 13. He's progressed to playing catch without any issues and is close to throwing a bullpen.
Right middle fingernail fell off after March 17 start. Threw four simulated innings to hitters on April 21 and is nearing a rehab assignment.
Was evaluated by Dr. Keith Meister in Texas and underwent season-ending ligament and flexor tendon repair surgery, the club announced April 8.
Not going to play in 2026 and had the last year of his contract deferred over several years. Was placed on 60-day IL on March 24.

110.status: Injury was sustained in days after April 18 start in Cleveland. Expected to miss at least several weeks.
Exited first inning of new rehab assignment with Triple-A Norfolk (his 14th overall rehab game) due to right hand discomfort. MRI, CT scans, X-rays all clean. Shut down to let inflammation subside.
Hospitalized in Pittsburgh during April 3-5 series. Received IV antibiotics for swelling. Allowed two runs in 3 1/3 innings over three rehab outings for Triple-A Norfolk from April 18-24.
Sustained injury during March 19 Grapefruit League game. Underwent imaging on March 23 that revealed low-grade strain. Doing full baseball activities in Sarasota, Fla., as of April 24.
After testing, received platelet-rich plasma injection on Feb. 20. Continues to hit and throw in Sarasota, Fla., as of April 24.
Got second opinion with Dr. Neal ElAttrache in Los Angeles on April 20. Has inflammation in shoulder joint. Felt soreness coming out of March 31 outing.
Exited April 11 game vs. Giants after stumbling between first and second and sliding awkwardly into the bag on a double. Broke fourth metatarsal on left foot. Expected to miss substantial time.
Exited 2026 debut on March 31 due to right elbow discomfort. After getting imaging and a second opinion, underwent Tommy John surgery on April 8.
Underwent surgery to repair torn rotator cuff and torn labrum last Aug. 19. Threw for first time March 3, tossing 25 pitches from 45 feet on flat ground.
Placed on the 60-day injured list on Feb. 14. No clear timeline for potential return.

111.status: Resumed playing catch and will throw bullpen session on April 27. Should be ready to come off IL when eligible on May 6.
Has been playing catch for last week or so. Could throw in the bullpen on April 28.

111.injury: Ruptured left patellar tendon/left abdominal strain
111.status: Rehab from left knee surgery went well, but now has left abdominal strain that will prolong return to action.
Imaging on right elbow revealed no structural damage. Waiting for flexor strain irritation to lessen before he can resume throwing.
Recalled from his Minor League rehab assignment due to discomfort in biceps area. No imaging scheduled at this point.
Consultation with Dr. Keith Meister revealed right flexor strain. Shut down from throwing for six weeks. Transferred to 60-day IL on April 14.

111.injury: Torn ulnar collateral ligament right elbow/right flexor pronator strain
111.status: Started gradual throwing program on flat ground in the second week of Spring Training.

112.status: Felt some discomfort in April 23 outing and did not recover well, per manager Craig Counsell. Will fly back to Chicago for further testing.

112.expectedReturn: During April 27-29 series at San Diego
112.status: Logged 25 pitches in one scoreless inning in a rehab outing with Triple-A Iowa on April 24. Rejoined Cubs in L.A. on April 25 and on target for activation soon.
Had a light "touch and feel" bullpen session on April 25. Next step will be a higher-intensity mound workout on April 28 in San Diego.
Injured hand while working out April 13. Had rehab outing for Triple-A Iowa on April 25 (23 pitches in one inning). Will have at least one more rehab game.

112.playerName: Jaxon Wiggins (Cubs' No. 1 prospect, No. 50 overall)
112.status: Last pitched on April 4 for Iowa. Moved to Minor League IL out of caution. Has resumed playing catch, per president of baseball operations Jed Hoyer.
Removed in ninth inning of series finale against Phillies on April 23. Cubs hoping the lefty will be able to return close to eligibility date.
Has resumed a throwing program, but still has several steps in his build back to pitching.
Underwent reconstruction of ulnar collateral ligament with an internal brace April 20 via Dr. Keith Meister. Initial timetable for return is 12-14 months.
Threw 43 pitches in 2 2/3 scoreless innings in second rehab outing with Triple-A Iowa on April 23. Had two strikeouts, one walk and five hits allowed.
Underwent a right UCL revision repair with an internal brace on April 16. Timetable for return is estimated around 15-16 months.

Medically cleared Feb. 28 by Dr. Keith Meister, who performed Steele's elbow surgery (left ulnar collateral ligament revision repair) on April 18, 2025. Has advanced to facing hitters in live BP sessions.
Underwent a right patellar tendon debridement on Feb. 24. Could miss months while recovering.
Underwent surgery to repair a torn right UCL and flexor tendon last October. Signed a two-year deal with Chicago on Feb. 16 with an eye toward contributing in 2027.

113.status: Was scratched from series opener against Detroit on April 24. Announced postgame that he would head to the IL with an oblique injury.

Faced hitters on April 24 in Cincinnati and felt good. Ferguson is scheduled to begin a rehab assignment that will begin April 28 with two games at Double-A Chattanooga before moving on to Triple-A Louisville for two more.
Threw 50 pitches in three simulated innings against hitters on April 21. Slated to bump up to four innings on a rehab assignment with High-A Dayton on April 26.
Had arthroscopic surgery March 11 to remove bone chips and loose bodies from elbow. Shut down from throwing for six weeks; expected to miss 14-16 weeks.

114.status: Felt issue while warming up in bullpen on April 24. Expected to be a minimum IL stint. Eligible to return on May 8.
Made sixth appearance of rehab assignment on April 24 with Triple-A Columbus
Exited early from Triple-A Columbus' game on April 22 due to issue. Issue considered mild.
Suffered injury running to second base while hitting a double in fifth inning on April 6. Imaging revealed moderate strain. Expected to miss four to eight weeks.

115.status: Last pitched on April 23, allowing two runs in two innings before exiting with tightness near the elbow area.
Threw a total of 41 pitches during a two-inning simulated game at Coors Field on April 22. Is eligible to return on April 28 in Cincinnati.
Scheduled to face hitters at the club's complex in Scottsdale on April 22, and if all goes well, will join in games in the Arizona Complex League

115.expectedReturn: To be determined after he is cleared for game action
115.status: Continues playing catch at the team's facility in Scottsdale, Ariz.

Underwent ligament transfer Tommy John surgery and internal brace procedure on March 4.
There is no return in sight for Bryant, who has had a long and painful time since signing with the Rockies before the 2022 season.

116.status: Left game April 25 after his ankle gave way on the mound after delivering a pitch, causing him to fall backward.
Threw an encouraging bullpen session on April 24 in Cincinnati. Will throw at least one more bullpen session next week in Atlanta before potentially progressing to a rehab assignment.
Completing a running progression and other baseball activity without limitation at Tigers' Spring Training complex in Lakeland, Fla. Will likely remain there until ready for a rehab assignment.
Threw latest bullpen session on April 24.
Threw a live session to hitters on April 23.
Completing rehab daily after receiving a cortisone injection in mid-April.

Resumed throwing program on April 9 following a scheduled rest period as part of his rehab. Played catch out to 120 feet over the past few weeks.
Received PRP injection on April 9 to address shoulder soreness after throwing during the week of April 1.
Placed on 7-day injured list at Triple-A Toledo on April 5.
Underwent surgery on Feb. 2, after offseason rehab program was unsuccessful.

117.status: Threw in the bullpen April 26 in Houston and is scheduled to make his first Minor League rehab outing April 28 at Double-A Corpus Christi.
Struck in the back by a line drive during his April 6 start. Was scheduled to make a rehab appearance April 26 at Triple-A Sugar Land.
Threw 25 pitches, hitting 91-93 mph with his fastball, during a live batting practice session April 25 at Daikin Park. He'll throw one more live BP before heading out on rehab.
Has started his throwing program by playing catch up to 75 feet.
Has resumed hitting and was on the field prior to the April 24 game against the Yankees fielding grounders and throwing to first base.
Threw two innings in a Minor League rehab assignment April 23 for Triple-A Sugar Land, allowing one hit, four walks and one run while striking out three on 31 strikes out of 53 pitches.
Should start a throwing program soon after exiting his April 8 start against Rockies with right shoulder tightness.
Performing light baseball activities as he prepares to start a running program.
Has begun light baseball activity after exiting the game against the Rockies on April 8 mid-plate appearance following a swing.
Exited after running the bases vs. the Guardians on April 20. Manager Joe Espada said it will "take some time for him to get back."
Manger Joe Espada said Allen will “need some time” after his back didn’t respond to treatment.

117.injury: Torn ulnar collateral ligament in right elbow
117.status: Has progressed to throwing in the bullpen in West Palm Beach after undergoing Tommy John surgery to repair the ulnar collateral ligament in his right elbow on May 23, 2025.

117.injury: Torn ulnar collateral ligament in left elbow
117.status: Underwent Tommy John surgery on Sept. 23, 2025.

118.status: Hit by a comebacker March 28. Threw a two-up bullpen April 25 and is heading to Arizona to face hitters on April 28. Manager Matt Quatraro didn't have exact number but said velocity was into the 90s.
Strained oblique Feb. 25. Began rehab assignment with Triple-A Omaha April 14. Threw 3 2/3 innings (74 pitches) April 24 and is scheduled for another outing week of April 27.
Has been battling valgus extension overload in elbow. Began rehab assignment with Triple-A Omaha April 15. Threw two scoreless innings April 25.
Has dealt with similar injury before, but got worse in last week or so. Received injection to help with pain, but is seeing a specialist week of April 27 to determine next steps.
After missing 2025 season with an elbow injury, has continued to have problems with soreness. Scheduled to see a specialist to determine next steps.
After missing 2025 with shoulder issues, underwent right labral surgery in October. Rehabbing in Surprise, Ariz., during Spring Training but likely out for year.

119.status: Was out of the starting lineup in consecutive games from April 25-26. Manager Dave Roberts said Smith is day to day and could return to the lineup on April 27.
Taking batting practice in the cage. Per manager Dave Roberts, Betts could begin a rehab assignment during the weekend of May 1-3 or early in the following week.
Threw 32 pitches across one-plus innings in his first rehab start with Single-A Ontario on April 22.
Had surgery on his elbow on April 22 and is expected to be out until after the All-Star break.
After two strong outings with Single-A Ontario, continuing his rehab assignment with Triple-A Oklahoma City.
Hitting upper 90s in his bullpen sessions at the Dodgers' complex in Arizona, per manager Dave Roberts.
Felt discomfort in his shoulder after his outing on April 12.
Imaging revealed inflammation, but not structural damage, in his shoulder. Has started a throwing program and essentially has an entire Spring Training ramp-up ahead.
Underwent Tommy John surgery in June 2025 and could be a second-half option for the Dodgers.
Tweaked his side while throwing in the bullpen on March 13. He will not begin a throwing program until he's pain-free.
Progressed to throwing off the mound by the end of Spring Training, but the team is building him up slowly.
Started throwing full bullpen sessions in mid-March.

120.status: Last pitched on April 21, throwing one scoreless inning in relief vs. the Braves.

Threw a 25-pitch bullpen on April 22 at the Nationals’ training complex in West Palm Beach, Fla.
Played catch to a distance of 120 feet on April 22; will travel to Nationals training complex to resume throwing progression.
Threw a 20-pitch bullpen on April 20; will travel to Nationals training complex to resume throwing progression.

120.playerName: Jarlin Susana (Nationals' No. 4 prospect, MLB No. 70)

120.playerName: Travis Sykora (Nationals' No. 2 prospect, MLB No. 48)
120.status: Continuing throwing progression, including throwing on flat ground three times a week.

121.status: Wrist soreness popped up April 14 in addition to his Achilles discomfort. Has been performing light baseball activities but remains in a week-to-week progression, according to Mets officials.
Strained calf April 22. Will be in a boot for a week and undergo a follow-up MRI the week of May 11. Will remain on a fluid timeline until he receives results.
Began a rehab assignment April 9 with Single-A St. Lucie, putting him on track to return May 9 at the latest. Likely to use most if not all of his 30-day rehab clock and return in early May.
Began feeling knee discomfort during an April 12 game against the Athletics. An April 14 MRI revealed a meniscus tear, requiring surgery. Had stitches removed the week of April 20.

Developed a blister during an April 13 appearance in Los Angeles. Shouldn't need more than the minimum 15 days to return, though his roster spot will depend on whether the Mets have a need once he's ready.
Exited the Mets' March 21 game after four innings due to knee discomfort and underwent an MRI, which revealed the tear. Will spend his recovery on the Minor League IL.
Fractured a rib during a March 5 game against the Nationals. Will need 4-8 weeks to recover, after which he can commence his buildup.
Will miss all of 2026 recovering from surgery but is under team control through 2029.
Will miss all of 2026 recovering from surgery but is under team control through 2027.
Will miss all of 2026 recovering from surgery but is under team control through 2029.

133.status: Exited in the middle of an at-bat after 3 1/3 scoreless on April 26 with what manager Mark Kotsay called "some form of a stinger in the shoulder area."

133.injury: Mid foot bone bruise in right foot
133.status: Injury stems from acute-on-chronic situation in his right big toe that he had been dealing with this season. A more clear timetable for his return will be provided at a later date.
Began a throwing progression April 19. No timetable for his return.

134.status: Began a rehab assignment with Single-A Bradenton on April 24. Playing seven innings on April 26. Had been progressing and ran basepaths on April 20.

General manager Ben Cherington said on a radio interview that Jones will begin a rehab assignment with Single-A Bradenton on April 29. He will make several outings there before his next steps are determined.

135.status: Landed on the IL after feeling "tightness" in his elbow playing catch April 10. Began a rehab stint on April 24, pitching a scoreless inning with Single-A Lake Elsinore.

Has made four rehab appearances with Triple-A El Paso, including one on April 22. Needs a buildup akin to a full Spring Training, per manager Craig Stammen, which could put him on track for an early May return.
Missed World Baseball Classic due to the injury, then began the season on the IL. Began rehab stint with Triple-A El Paso April 4 and could be nearing a return.
Only appeared in five Cactus League games after sustaining the injury in early March. Playing extended Spring Training games at the team's complex in Arizona.
Underwent surgery in October 2024. Was hopeful for an early season return, but experienced a setback after making one start in Spring Training. Playing light catch but has yet to throw off the mound.

Exited April 12 start with elbow stiffness; shut down from throwing. MRI revealed flexor strain. Expected to pitch again this season, though manager Craig Stammen noted Pivetta's absence would last "weeks and maybe months."
Underwent surgery to repair the flexor tendon in his right elbow on March 17 and will miss the 2026 season.

Underwent an internal brace procedure on his right elbow on Oct. 29, which comes with a recovery timetable of 12-15 months. Will miss the 2026 season and was placed on the restricted list on March 25.
Underwent UCL internal brace surgery as well as a flexor tendon repair operation in April 2025. Resumed throwing bullpens in Padres camp.

136.status: Held out of April 26 game as a precaution, but was available off the bench.More »
Miller made his second rehab start on April 24, throwing three scoreless innings for High-A Everett. He struck out six on 47 pitches, and his fastball was sitting at 96-98 mph.More »
Exited April 17 game after tweaking the area while making a play in the field, missed next two games, then placed on IL after undergoing imaging that revealed a more severe issue.More »
Out of lineup on April 19, but issue is not expected to be serious and should return in coming days.
Suffered the injury while playing for Team Italy at World Baseball Classic. Began rehab assignment at Triple-A Tacoma on April 8, but was transferred to 60-day IL on April 20.

Was still a few weeks away from beginning to throw at the time of GM Justin Hollander's latest update. Will remain with club in Seattle during next road trip. Suffered injury during outfielders’ drills on April 7.
Began catch play on April 16 but won't begin full buildup until late May. Transferred to 60-day IL on April 8. Suffered injury during catch play leading up to Opening Day.
Suffered the injury during brief callup from April 14-16 in San Diego, after which he underwent an MRI that revealed the issue.
Underwent surgery to repair his right ulnar collateral ligament in January, and will be out all season.More »

137.status: Has given up four runs over 5 1/3 innings (6.75 ERA) in his first six rehab appearances with Triple-A Sacramento.
Threw three scoreless innings in his first three rehab appearances with Triple-A Sacramento.
Expected to miss two to three weeks after experiencing soreness in his throwing elbow during the Giants' series in Washington from April 17-19.
Underwent a second surgery to repair an axillary artery in his upper right arm on April 17, which will sideline him for five to six months.
Began a throwing program on April 6 and is currently tossing bullpens in Arizona.
Sidelined with a hamstring issue that had been bothering him since the last week of Spring Training.
Broke his hamate on a swing on April 14; expected to miss six to eight weeks after undergoing surgery to remove the bone from his wrist on April 16.
Underwent season-ending Tommy John surgery with Dr. Keith Meister on March 25.
Underwent surgery to repair the anterior capsule in his right shoulder in May 2025. Started a bullpen progression on Feb. 10 and is throwing twice per week.
Expected to miss 12-14 months after undergoing Tommy John surgery on Sept. 24, 2025. Was slated to begin a throwing progression in mid-March.
Expected to miss the entire 2026 season after undergoing Tommy John surgery in November 2025.

138.status: Pitched two innings for Triple-A Memphis at Norfolk on April 21, allowing one run on two hits and a walk.
Made third rehab start for Triple-A Memphis on April 14 and is expected to throw again on April 25.
Underwent surgery last October to address Haglund’s deformities in both heels. Started his running progression on March 20, but no date has been set for a return to the lineup.

139.status: Joined Triple-A Durham to start a rehab assignment on April 19 and has made three appearances, allowing four runs in two innings with two strikeouts. Another outing scheduled for April 26.
Sustained a Grade 2 left ankle sprain while running the bases for Triple-A Durham on April 22. Imaging revealed no fracture. Goal is to return to game action in four to six weeks.
Sprained ankle running to first base April 11 while on rehab assignment. Returned to Triple-A Durham's lineup on April 24.

After rocky rehab outing for Triple-A Durham April 15, reported soreness in back of right shoulder. Further evaluation revealed a new injury, a subscapularis strain. Shut down for 2-3 weeks, meaning he's likely 4-6 weeks away from returning.

Threw a 25-pitch bullpen on April 12, then received an injection in his hip while being transferred to the 60-day IL. Felt "unstable" playing catch from 60 feet on April 20, his first day throwing after the injection, but plans to continue throwing program.
Imaging revealed no structural damage, and manager Kevin Cash called the IL move cautionary. Playing catch from 90 feet and hoping to throw a bullpen by the end of April.
Will be shut down from throwing for three to four days. Imaging revealed no serious injury, so not concerned about long-term effects. Will be built back up to cover multiple innings.
Signed to a Major League contract on April 14 and immediately placed on the IL. Was about three weeks away from facing hitters at that time.
Underwent surgery to repair a right elbow flexor strain on Aug. 8. Hopes to return in June or July. Playing catch with no issues.
Did not pitch all spring while dealing with a back issue that required an injection. Will resume throwing in early April, but will need a while to build up after being shut down so long.

140.status: Had another setback in throwing program. Will continue to be no-throw for at least another few days. Hopes to throw plyo balls early next week. Threw 27 pitches in rehab appearance with Triple-A Round Rock April 2. Has not pitched since.
Was up to 97 mph in one inning of a rehab appearance with Triple-A Round Rock on April 23. Will need to throw on back-to-back days before he returns to the big league club.
Examined by team doctors on April 20 for shoulder soreness and was placed on the IL on April 23.
Left an April 21 win over the Pirates with right forearm tightness and underwent an MRI the following morning, showing a mild strain. The Rangers expect him to miss the minimum amount of days.
Only threw one inning in an April 14 loss to the A’s due to arm fatigue that was eventually classified as shoulder impingement.
Left Tuesday night’s loss to the A’s after throwing two pitches due to feeling something in his arm. Scans showed minimal issues, though he will be down for at least two weeks.
Down four to six weeks minimum before re-evaluation.
Signed to a Major League deal on Feb. 13, though is continuing to rehab from Tommy John surgery. Should be back on a big league mound around the Trade Deadline.

141.status: "Week to week" after an MRI revealed a left hamstring strain from running the bases.

Transferred to 60-day IL on April 25 as he continues to throw and build up slowly in Dunedin.
Will pitch Tuesday with Triple-A Buffalo in what could be his final rehab outing.
In early stages of his own "Spring Training" ramp-up and will continue to face live hitters.
Remains in Florida. Manager John Schneider did not have an immediate update on his throwing status but mentioned he would be checking on it.
Discovered after Estrada was optioned, so he has been placed on the Major League IL.
Underwent surgery and is expected to miss the rest of 2026.
Underwent surgery on April 7 and had a screw inserted in his thumb. Expectation is a six-week recovery.
Underwent surgery to repair the labral tear and is hoping to return to the Blue Jays in five to six months. This timeline should be clearer by June, but any return in 2026 would be a success.
Underwent surgery and will miss the entire 2026 season.

142.status: Has advanced to Triple-A St. Paul as he continues rehab assignment. Will pitch multi-inning relief outings.
Was scheduled to throw live batting practice before the end of this past week. Will throw multiple live BPs before progressing into games.

143.status: He is scheduled to begin a rehab assignment on April 26 with Triple-A Lehigh Valley.

Throwing bullpen sessions in Clearwater, Fla. Left Team Israel in the World Baseball Classic after feeling something in his abdomen/oblique. Transferred to 60-day IL on April 22.

143.playerName: Aidan Miller (Phillies' No. 1 prospect, No. 18 overall)

He is playing catch. He felt something in his calf while doing agility work on April 14.

144.status: Left April 23 game in the seventh inning after feeling quad tightness while running to second base on an RBI double. He was a late scratch from the April 24 lineup.
The left-handed reliever felt discomfort after his April 21 appearance.
Looked like his dominant self while recording eight strikeouts over 4 1/3 scoreless innings for Triple-A Gwinnett on April 21. He may need just one more rehab start.
Caught six innings for Triple-A Gwinnett on April 21. There is a chance he'll be activated within the next week.
Iglesias slept on his shoulder wrong on April 17. When he made his next appearance, on April 19, his velocity was slightly down. He’ll be eligible for activation on May 5.

Underwent surgery on his right elbow on June 9.
Will miss the 2026 season after tearing his right ACL during a collision at first base during a Grapefruit League game on March 8.
Underwent arthroscopic surgery to remove bone spurs from his elbow on Feb. 18. There is a chance he could return in June or July, but the team isn’t putting a timetable on it.
Missed 2025 while recovering from left knee surgery and he had a cleanup procedure in November. There is no timetable for his return.

145.status: Will begin a rehab assignment with Triple-A Charlotte on April 28.
Teel is making progress, but still has not crossed the final threshold for an injury rehab assignment.
Hays will begin an injury rehab assignment with Triple-A Charlotte on April 25.
Scheduled to begin an injury rehab assignment with Triple-A Charlotte on April 25.
Underwent season-ending Tommy John surgery with the procedure performed by Dr. Keith Meister, but was with the team for the home opener.
Placed on 60-day IL as he continues to recover from Tommy John surgery he underwent last year. Has thrown numerous times off the mound.
Underwent successful surgery to repair the torn ACL on Oct. 28. Estimated recovery time is eight months, per the team.
Continues with the bullpen portion of his recovery ramp-up, having thrown seven. Has felt great as the rehab process moves forward.

146.status: Making steady progress in a rehab assignment with Triple-A Jacksonville that began April 21. Manager Clayton McCullough said Morel needs more playing time and did not offer a date for his return.
Slated to undergo left hamstring tendon excision week of April 13. Felt discomfort after making diving attempt during Marlins' win over Reds on April 9.

146.playerName: Aiva Arquette (Marlins' No. 3 prospect, No. 41 overall)
146.status: Underwent core muscle surgery on Feb. 27. Sustained left groin injury during offseason workouts. Rested before arriving in Jupiter, where it flared up again.

Made second rehab start on April 23, throwing 42 of 52 pitches for strikes in 4 1/3 innings for High-A Hudson Valley. Is expected to build up pitch count over additional rehab starts.
Made first rehab start on April 24 for High-A Hudson Valley, throwing 65 pitches (43 strikes) over 4 1/3 scoreless innings. Will likely make two more rehab starts.
Was getting treatment after shoulder began bothering him while Yankees were in Boston, then hit IL when he had difficulty throwing April 25 in Houston. Will head back to New York for testing.

Had his Minor League rehab assignment transferred to Triple-A Scranton/Wilkes-Barre on April 21 after going 4-for-11 (.364) with Double-A Somerset. Will play nine innings April 24 and 25, then DH on April 26.
Threw 10 pitches from a mound April 10, his first time doing so since Tommy John surgery in July 2025. Could face hitters in June and is on track for MLB activation sometime in August.

158.status: Hit on the field for the first time on April 24 and has his next medical check-up on April 28, at which time Vaughn hopes to be cleared for a rehab assignment. (last updated: April 24)
Initial hope is that he can avoid surgery and recover with treatment. Was not yet throwing as of an April 24 update (last updated: April 24)

Sent to Triple-A Nashville on rehab assignment on April 22 and recorded only two outs, throwing 12 of 29 pitches for strikes. Tentatively has rehab starts scheduled for April 28 and May 3 before the Brewers reassess. (last updated: April 22)
Resumed hitting on the field on April 22 in Detroit and will be reassessed after the weekend of April 24-26 regarding his readiness for a rehab assignment. (last updated: April 22)

Returned from his rehab assignment on April 4 after suffering a rib-cage injury while coming back from a shoulder injury. Was playing catch at 90 feet as of an April 24 update. (last updated: April 14)
Suffered a second-degree left adductor strain in the Brewers' April 12 loss to the Nationals. (last updated: April 14)
Injured running to first base on March 9, and an MRI revealed a more serious injury than initially believed. (last updated: March 25)
